name: Squash Dressing (Mine)
recipeInstructions:
I am sorry about the amounts...I haven't measured the ingredients, but after the first time you will have a better idea of how much to use.
Make a 9-inch skillet of cornbread. Let cool. Take about 4-8 yellow squash (depending on size, about 2-3 lbs) and boil until soft. While the squash is cooking, put the cornbread in a bowl and cut with a butter knife (dull bladed dinner knife) until crumbly. Melt butter in skillet, sauté vegetables until soft, let cool. Mix soup with eggs (I use between 3 and 6 eggs depending on how it looks). Add cooled veggies, stir well. Stir into cornbread. Add squash and enough water to achieve the consistency of oatmeal (lumpy and soupy at the same time). Stir in salt and pepper. Cut cheese into 1-inch cubes and stir in. People use between 1/2 lb and 1 lb of cheese depending on how much they like it. I use mild Mexican, but others will prefer hot. Pour/slide into baking dish allowing at least 1-2 inches of growing room. Bake at 350°F until done, lightly brown, and not runny (from the eggs). Allow to settle for 10-15 minutes.
